paragraphs.body: Title: The cultural similarities and differences between Pittsburgh and my hometown, Beijing Main point: I want to convey the three main points between two places, The difference of architectural.
style; the mapping of historical background; the similarities and differences of living habits.

Exposition：I have never been to Schenley Park in Pittsburgh. Before I went there, my impression of Schenley Park is that there are small rivers, various paths, various landscapes of flowers and trees. “青山隐隐水迢迢，秋尽江南草未凋“There are also relevant interpreters in the scenic area to take you to visit, people take a leisurely walk here, and old people take children for a walk here. My expectation of Schenley Park is that I can take a quiet walk here, away from the hustle and bustle of the city, and enjoy the embrace of nature. The key elements of my preconception is that park is a scenic spot for out-of-town tourists to visit and appreciate.

Conflict：In fact, after my first visit to Schenley Park, I found that it was not what I had imagined before, there were large empty lawns, there were some wild animals such as squirrels and deer walking on the lawns, and there were many young people chatting and eating picnics on the lawns. “落霞与孤鹜齐飞，秋水共长天一色”There are some details that struck me, I saw the people here to protect the ecology so well, people and natural animals live in harmony, do not disturb each other makes me amazed.

Rising action：Compared with my hometown, I think Beijing Olympic Forest Park and Schenley Park are very similar, both have very lush vegetation, and there are a lot of animals co-existing in the park, people camping in the park, running and doing sports; “锦江春色来天地，玉 垒浮云变古今“However, the difference is that the scenic spots in Beijing are occupied by a large number of tourists, and there are many guides explaining. In terms of architecture, Shenley Park is mainly dominated by some western churches and Gothic buildings, which are generally older, while Beijing's Olympic Forest Park is mainly dominated by modern buildings, including the five Ring towers with many Olympic elements. The overall feeling is that Schenley Park is more comfortable and relaxed atmosphere, and Olympic Forest Park is more crowded.

Climax：Cross-cultural comparison makes me change the way of thinking about these two places. By studying the cultural backgrounds of the two countries, I find that the population of the United States is small, and people tend to live outdoors; while the population of China is large, and once there is a scenic spot, a large number of people will go to visit it. Second, the history of economic development and construction in the United States is relatively early, preserving many buildings from the developed period of a hundred years ago, while China developed relatively late, so the buildings in most developed areas are relatively new.

Resolution：This comparison enables me to have a deeper understanding of the cultural diversity between the two places from the perspectives of historical background, economic development, people's usual living habits, and the style of atmosphere and environment.
